findings.title: Header *name* is never validated — only the value is checked
findings.detail: The code validates only `val` against the pattern but never validates `name`. A caller can craft a header name containing `\r\n` to perform header injection via the key (e.g. `{'foo\r\nEvil: injected': 'bar'}`). RFC 7230 prohibits CR/LF in field names just as much as in field values. The same regex (or a stricter token-only check) should be applied to `name` as well.

findings.title: Regex compiled on every `prepare_headers` call — should be a module-level constant
findings.detail: `re.compile(r'^\S[^\r\n]*$|^$')` is created inside `prepare_headers` (and inside the `if headers:` branch) on every single invocation. It should be hoisted to a module-level constant (e.g. `INVALID_HEADER_PAT = re.compile(r'^\S[^\r\n]*$|^$')`) to avoid repeated compilation overhead and to make the intent visible at a glance.

findings.title: Trailing newline bypasses `$` anchor — use `\Z` instead
`None` header values cause an unhelpful `TypeError` instead of silent removal
findings.detail: It is an established Requests idiom to pass `None` as a header value to signal removal (e.g. `headers={'X-Foo': None}`). With the new code, `to_native_string(None)` raises a `TypeError`/`AttributeError` before the `InvalidHeader` check, producing a confusing traceback. A guard such as `if val is None: self.headers[name] = val; continue` should be added before the validation.

findings.title: `InvalidHeader` not re-exported from the top-level `requests` package
findings.detail: `InvalidHeader` is defined in `requests.exceptions` and is used in tests and models, but it is not added to `requests/__init__.py`'s imports. Users expecting to do `from requests import InvalidHeader` or catch `requests.InvalidHeader` (consistent with every other public exception like `InvalidURL`, `MissingSchema`, etc.) will get an `AttributeError`. The exception should be added to the `__init__.py` exports.

findings.title: 'Tab' test case contains spaces, not a tab character
findings.detail: The test labelled `#test for tab` at line 1168 uses `{'foo': '   bar'}` (three spaces), not a tab (`\t`). The label is misleading. A real tab character `{'foo': '\tbar'}` should be used to verify that `\t` as leading whitespace is also rejected by the `\S` check.

findings.title: Header validation tests unnecessarily depend on live `httpbin` fixture
findings.detail: The three new tests (`test_header_validation`, `test_header_no_return_chars`, `test_header_no_leading_space`) all accept the `httpbin` parameter and make real network calls, but header validation fires in `prepare_headers` before any network I/O. These should use `requests.Request(...).prepare()` with a dummy URL instead, making them fast unit tests that don't depend on the test server being available.

findings.title: Missing blank line between `InvalidURL` and `InvalidHeader` class definitions
findings.detail: PEP 8 requires two blank lines between top-level class definitions. There is no blank line between the end of `InvalidURL` (line 83) and `InvalidHeader` (line 85), inconsistent with the rest of the file.

findings.title: Missing space after comma in function signature
findings.detail: `def test_header_validation(self,httpbin):` is missing a space after the comma, inconsistent with the surrounding test code style.
